name: Cockroach Cove (Halona Cove)
tagline: The tiny hidden cove below the Halona Blowhole that most tourists photograph but never swim.
parking: Free lot at Halona Blowhole Lookout; short steep scramble down to the cove
bestTime: Summer (May–Sep) when south shore is calm; ONLY on flat days — waves funnel into this cove dangerously
talkStory: Halona means 'peering place' in Hawaiian — ancient fishermen watched for fish schools from these cliffs long before Hollywood filmed From Here to Eternity here in 1953. The cove's lava shelters are packed with wana (sea urchins) and juvenile goatfish that use it as a nursery. If the Blowhole across the road is shooting spray, the cove has dangerous surge — only enter when it's dead silent and you can see the sandy bottom clearly.
